question: Two-flavor lattice QCD (N_f=2) is simulated with a one-loop Symanzik-improved gauge action and Asqtad staggered fermion action on lattices with temporal extent L_t=4 and spatial volumes L_s=12,16,20. The model exhibits Roberge-Weiss (RW) phase transitions at imaginary chemical potential mu=i*mu_I at theta=(2n+1)pi/3. Finite-size scaling of the Polyakov-loop susceptibility and Binder cumulant B_4 are used to classify the RW endpoint at bare quark mass am=0.038. What is the ORDER of the Roberge-Weiss transition endpoint at am=0.038? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". If the paper identifies the universality class, include "universality_class" using one of: "3D Ising", "tricritical", "mean-field", "other".

metadata.system: Lattice QCD N_f=2 with imaginary chemical potential, Roberge-Weiss endpoint at am=0.038
metadata.source_excerpt: at am=0.026, 0.038, 0.040 the finite-volume behavior and fitted exponents are consistent with a second-order RW endpoint in the three-dimensional Ising universality class
metadata.difficulty_note: Mass-dependent transition order; intermediate masses (0.026-0.040) are 3D Ising second-order while small/large masses are first-order triple points.
